patch_description: Proposes a more expressive yet parameter-efficient ansatz by introducing per-edge hub entanglement via CRX gates. This preserves the data re-uploading structure and symmetry-aware pre/post rotations, ring CZ entanglement, and vanila single-qubit gates, but adds four separate CRX gates between the center qubit (8) and each edge (1,3,5,7) with individual parameters. This increases expressivity while maintaining adjacency constraints and the same overall block structure, aiming to improve validation/test performance and reduce overfitting by using a modest increase in parameter count (N_PARAMS_PER_BLOCK goes from 10 to 13, total N_PARAMS from 60 to 78). Parameter sharing is still used for the RZ/RY layers to leverage board symmetry, while hub-edge CRX gates receive distinct parameters to capture edge-specific couplings.
